title: DOF Statewide Fire Roundup for August 16th, 2025
summary: This will be the final update for the Division of Forestry & Fire Protection (DOF) fires this Alaska fire season unless there is significant activity. While repair work on the affected areas will continue as the season winds down, anyone with specific questions can call the Statewide Fire Information line at (907) 356-5511. Tok Area: 7 Mile Lookout Fire (#222) 4,095 acres I Containment: 58% I Total Personnel: 60 Update: Crews are continuing to work on the southern and eastern sides of the fire, focusing on cooling previously identified heat sources. The Tanana Chiefs Fire Crew and the Mat-Su Crew are supporting suppre...

title: AST Hit and Run: Wasilla [AK25081728]
summary: On 08/14/2025 AST received a report of a hit and run at 3 bears Pittman. AST located the vehicle on 08/16/2025 in a parking lot in Wasilla, and contacted the driver, identified as Victor Gassaway, age 56, of Wasilla. Victor was issued a Misdemeanor summons and released.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: 2025 Mendenhall GLOF
summary: This year’s glacial lake outburst flood originating from the glacier damned lake in Suicide Basin occurred on August 13. The water level of the Mendenhall River at Auke Bay reached 16.65 feet, a new record that tops even the previous records set in 2023 and 2024. The water level of the crest was pre...

title: Arctic Sea Ice Extent: August 14, 2025
summary: August 14, 2025: The Arctic sea ice has experienced a greater decrease rate for this week of 7.25% (last week’s decrease rate was 5.64%), from the level on 08/07/2025 of 5.859 M km 2 to 5.434 M km 2 for this week. The sea ice extent for 2025 to date has slipped to 4th place for the lowest sea ice ex...

title: AST Assault 4: Nenana [AK25082286]
summary: On 08/15/2025, Alaska State Troopers responded to an address in the Nenana area of Alaska regarding a disturbance. The investigation resulted in a bevy of charges being forwarded to the District Attorney's Office.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: resulted in a bevy of charges being forwarded to the District Attorney's Office

title: AST Fail to record catch P/U permit: Kasilof [AK25062638]
incident_type: Fail to record catch P/U permit
summary: On 6/28/2025 at approximately 0959 hours, Alaska Wildlife Troopers were conducting a P/U patrol on the Kasilof Beach. Derek Canute, age 55, from Wasilla, was contacted in the area. An investigation revealed that Canute failed to record his P/U fish on his 2025 Upper Cook Inlet Permit. A bailable cit...
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Sport Fishing General Seasons Regulations: Kasilof [AK25062972]
summary: On 6/28/2025 at approximately 0100 hours, Alaska Wildlife Troopers received a report from Marcelo Moya, age 38, from Anchorage, calling to turn himself in for catching and killing a Native King Salmon on the Kasilof River. subSources.incident_type: Commercial Fishing Closed Period

subSources.title: AST Commercial Fishing Closed Period: Kenai [AK25071638]
subSources.incident_type: Commercial Fishing Closed Period
subSources.summary: On 7/19/2025 at approximately 1442 hours, Alaska Wildlife Troopers were conducting a patrol in Cook Inlet during a Commercial Fishing Drift opener. Investigation revealed Perrin Dejax, age 26, from Homer, was fishing in a closed area. A mandatory court citation was filed with the Kenai Courthouse.

title: AmeriCorps is under siege. What happens in the communities it serves?
summary: Former AmeriCorps service member Daniel Zare, 27, visits Project Change at Sligo Middle School on Monday, Aug. 11, 2025 in Silver Spring, Maryland, where he mentored students before federal government cuts in April. (Photo by Ashley Murray/States Newsroom) SILVER SPRING, Md. — Daniel Zare worked one-on-one as an AmeriCorps member with students going through rough times in school, lightening teachers’ workload in the classroom. At AmeriCorps Project CHANGE, based in Silver Spring’s Sligo Middle School, Zare was one of several in his group who tracked adolescents’ emotional and social wellbeing over months using a system dubbed “My Score.” They then helped support the kids who were struggling the most. In April, though, the program screeched to a halt. That’s when the Trump administration abruptly canceled nearly $400 million in active AmeriCorps grants across the United States that fund volunteers who embed in communities, in exchange for a small stipend and education award. “All the wo...

title: Protests, along with some support, greet Trump-Putin summit in Alaska
summary: Ivan Hodes, a U.S. Army veteran, shows the bracelet he wears in memory of a West Point classmate who died in Ukraine. Hodes was standing on Aug. 15, 2025, at the Government Hill gate of Joint Base Elemdorf-Richardson, participating in a protest against the summit taking place later in the day between President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin. (Photo by Yereth Rosen/Alaska Beacon) Supporters of Ukraine hold the edges of a 136-foot flag at a protest held Aug. 15, 2025, on the Delaney Park Strip in downtown Anchorage. The gathering was one of several in Anchorage and elsewhere in Alaska protesting President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin, who held a summit that day at Anchorage...

title: Alaska state officials express hope about Trump-Putin summit
summary: A plane carrying Russian President Vladimir Putin arrives at Joint Base Elmendorf Richardson on Aug. 15, 2025, in Anchorage, Alaska. President Trump is meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin for peace talks aimed at ending the war in Ukraine. (Photo by Andrew Harnik/Getty Images) Alaska’s top elected officials expressed hope about President Donald Trump’s...

title: Juneau flooding sparks days-long cell service outages in Haines and Skagway
summary: An AT&T spokesperson said in an email exchange earlier this week that service disruptions were related to flooding in Juneau and a fiber cut caused by a contractor. The post Juneau flooding sparks days-long cell service outages in Haines and Skagway appeared first on KHNS Radio | KHNS FM .

title: Haines Assembly makes borough manager permanent, chooses seasonal tax proposal and seeks more dock funding
summary: The Haines Borough Assembly grappled with two major financial challenges at its meeting on August 12 – the deteriorating Lutak Dock project and proposals for a new seasonal sales tax to address the borough’s budget shortfall. KHNS’ Brandon Wilks has more on the meeting that covered a six-page agenda...
